business.headline: FDs, SCSS or mutual funds? The best way to invest Rs 50 lakh after retirementRetiring with Rs 50 lakh may sound reassuring, but the real challenge begins after retirement: how do you turn that lump sum into a steady monthly income without running out of money?

US Justice Department okays Paramount's $110 billion Warner Bros takeoverAfter an eight-month review, the DOJ concluded the proposed merger would strengthen competition across streaming, television and film, potentially benefiting consumers, workers and the broader entertainment industry.

SpaceX opens at $150 in strong Nasdaq debut, up 11% from IPO priceSpaceX surged more than 20 per cent in its Nasdaq debut, lifting its valuation above USD 2 trillion as investors piled into the world's biggest IPO. The stock opened at USD 150, signalling strong demand and easing concerns over its market debut.

Groceries, fuel, school fees: Is your monthly budget about to get tighter?School fees, petrol, vegetables, utility bills—the list of monthly expenses never seems to end. As inflation rises, many families are asking the same question: is their budget about to get even tighter?

Inflation rises to 3.93% for May as West Asia conflict pushes up fuel and food pricesFood inflation continued to move higher in May. It stood at 4.78%, up from 4.20% in April, showing that prices of food items are rising faster than they were a month earlier.

Markets rally on easing West Asia tensions, oil falling below $88The S&P BSE Sensex surged 1,695.40 points, or 2.30%, to close at 75,527.95, while the NSE Nifty50 jumped 461.30 points, or 1.99%, to settle at 23,622.90.

Salary mismatch to missed interest: 7 ITR mistakes that can trigger tax noticesFiling your ITR may seem straightforward, but even a minor reporting error can attract the attention of the tax department. Here are seven mistakes you should watch out for.

The world's biggest IPO starts trading today. Is Elon Musk's SpaceX overvalued?SpaceX begins trading on Nasdaq after a record $75 billion IPO and a $1.75 trillion valuation. The debut has sharpened questions over valuation, Musk's control, xAI losses and broader market liquidity.

FD rates up to 8.10%: Check these small finance banks offering the highest returnsIn a world of rising uncertainty, guaranteed returns are becoming more valuable. Here's a look at the small finance banks offering FD rates as high as 8.10% this month.

Govt's new petrol, diesel order: Who is affected, and why was it introduced?The Centre has restricted industrial and commercial bulk fuel purchases from retail pumps. The move aims to protect pump supplies for ordinary consumers amid a wide retail-bulk price gap.

Earning Rs 20 lakh? Claim these tax benefits under the new tax regimeThink the new tax regime leaves no room for tax savings? If you earn Rs 20 lakh a year, you may be surprised to learn that several tax-friendly benefits are still available.
Gold demand picks up despite PM's appeal to avoid buyingAt the time of writing at 11.45 am, MCX gold was trading at Rs 1,49,942, up Rs 1,021, while silver stood at Rs 2,42,232, higher by Rs 2,579.

India may let fiscal deficit widen as US-Iran war raises oil costsIndia may allow its fiscal deficit to widen to 4.8% of GDP as the Iran war raises oil-linked costs. The move reflects pressure from higher subsidies, costlier imports and possible spending cuts.

Inflation fears return: Will Indians have to spend more on everyday items?Rising global oil prices and stronger US inflation are reviving concerns over India's household costs. Economists and the RBI say risks are building even without an immediate inflation shock.

Sensex jumped 1,700 points: 3 reasons why stock market rose todayThe S&P BSE Sensex surged 1,695.40 points, or 2.30%, to close at 75,527.95, while the NSE Nifty50 jumped 461.30 points, or 1.99%, to settle at 23,622.90.

Markets surge as crude oil falls below $90 amid hopes of US-Iran breakthroughThe S&P BSE Sensex surged 873.88 points, or 1.18%, to 74,706.43 in early trade, while the NSE Nifty50 climbed 236.40 points, or 1.02%, to 23,398.00.

World's first trillionaire Elon Musk is now richer than Taiwan after SpaceX IPOElon Musk became the world's first trillionaire after SpaceX's record IPO lifted his holdings. The milestone pushed his fortune past Taiwan's economy and widened the gap with every other billionaire.

Centre restricts bulk industrial fuel purchases at petrol pumpsThe government has barred industrial, commercial and institutional users from buying petrol and diesel at retail pumps. The move seeks to prevent diversion, curb shortages and protect supplies for ordinary vehicle owners.

US drug agency puts Dabur products on import alert after inspection flags lapsesThe US FDA has placed drugs manufactured at Dabur India's Dadra and Nagar Haveli factory under an import alert after an inspection found data integrity and maintenance lapses.

Steady adjustment of rupee is okay, a free fall is not: Raghuram RajanIn an exclusive interview with India Today TV, former Reserve Bank of India Governor Raghuram Rajan shares his thoughts on how to deal with the ongoing conflict in West Asia's impact on the global and Indian economies and more.

entertainment.headline: BTS at 13: What seven Korean men and millions of ARMYs taught me about life BTS celebrate their 13th debut anniversary on June 13, 2026. They will be performing in Busan. Tracing the life lessons learnt from BTS and their 13-year journey. Their story resonates deeply with fans, inspiring millions across India to embrace vulnerability and self-love while chasing dreams.

Pranit More issues fresh apology over Rs 370 biryani row Stand-up comedian Pranit More has issued a public apology following massive social media outrage over a viral video featuring a misogynistic remark made by an audience member.

Main Vaapas Aaunga box office day 1: Imtiaz Ali film trails behind Love Aaj Kal 2 Imtiaz Ali's Main Vaapas Aaunga grossed Rs 1.38 crore on its opening day. The modest start left it well below Love Aaj Kal 2 as the film heads into the weekend.

I deserve hate: Pranit More issues fresh apology over Rs 370 biryani row Pranit More has issued a fresh apology over Rs 370 biryani row after backlash over his longer viral video from his controversial event.

David Beckham gets Walk of Fame star, Tom Cruise calls his journey a Hollywood story Tom Cruise praised David Beckham as he received his star on the Hollywood Walk of Fame. He compared Beckham's journey, family and legacy to a true Hollywood story.

25 years later, I watched Lagaan for the first time. It still hits for six Twenty-five years later, I watched Lagaan and it still feels astonishingly alive. What could have easily become a relic of another era remains a deeply moving story about hope, community, nation-building and standing up against impossible odds.

Farhan Akhtar to headline RD Burman biopic by Neeraj Pandey amid Don 3 row? Farhan Akhtar is reportedly in talks to play RD Burman in a musical biopic directed by Neeraj Pandey. The upcoming project could mark his next major film even as the Don 3 dispute remains unresolved.

Pranit More row explained: How viral stand-up clip spiralled into national issue A crowd-work clip from Pranit More's stand-up show set off outrage after an audience member linked a Rs 370 biryani bill to sexual entitlement. The fallout widened into apologies, job loss, official action and a broader debate on consent and accountability.

Bharat Bhhagya Viddhaata box office Day 1: Kangana Ranaut film opens on slow note Actor Kangana Ranaut's Bharat Bhhagya Viddhaata opened with modest collections on a crowded Friday at the box office. Its weekend run will now hinge on word-of-mouth in a nine-film clash.

Haunted 3D review: Familiar ghost story that works better than expectedRelease Date:Sun Jul 12, 2026 Vikram Bhatt's Haunted 3D: Echoes of the Past follows a betrayed director into a cursed palace and a ghostly romance. The film's mystery holds attention, but weak VFX and minimal scares limit its impact.

Aamir Khan's Lagaan team reunites ahead of film's 25th anniversary Actor Aamir Khan and filmmaker Ashutosh Gowariker reunited with several members of the Lagaan cast ahead of the film's 25th anniversary. The gathering celebrated the legacy of the 2001 classic.

Ahaan Panday, Aneet Padda's matching star tattoos spark fan frenzy Fresh pictures of Ahaan Panday and Aneet Padda have sparked talk of matching neck tattoos. The viral sighting has intensified fan frenzy around the Saiyaara pair and their next film together.

India Ke Top 1% promo out: Anil Kapoor hunts for bright minds Star Plus has released the first promo of India Ke Top 1%, hosted by Anil Kapoor. The teaser presents the show as a logic-led contest that rewards common sense and calm under pressure.

Welcome to the Jungle will hopefully be another Khakee for me: Tusshar Kapoor Actor Tusshar Kapoor expressed hope that Welcome to the Jungle could be another Khakee moment in his career. He said director Ahmed Khan's handling of the giant cast gives every character room to stand out.

Dhamaal 4 trailer: Ajay Devgn rides flying dolphins, iconic cast teases comic chaos The Dhamaal 4 trailer introduces Ajay Devgn and the ensemble cast in a chaotic treasure hunt centred on a mountain of gold. It signals a bigger-scale return for the franchise as it begins its run-up to the film release.

Taylor Swift inducted into Songwriters Hall of Fame, thanks family in tearful speech Taylor Swift thanked her family and Steven Spielberg as she accepted her Songwriters Hall of Fame honour in New York. The speech framed her record-setting induction at 36 as a reflection on sacrifice, storytelling and endurance.

Box office: Governor vs Main Vaapas vs Bharat Bhhagya and why Haunted 3D is surprise The industry witnessed an unusually crowded box-office clash on Friday as nine films hit theatres simultaneously. With Governor, Main Vaapas Aaunga and Bharat Bhhagya Viddhaata leading the race, trade experts believe the weekend winner will ultimately be decided by word-of-mouth rather than star power.

Gram Chikitsalay 2 trailer: Amol Parashar's health mission faces new hurdles The trailer of Gram Chikitsalay 2 sends Dr Prabhat, played by Amol Parashar, back to Bhathkandi, where reviving the Primary Health Centre remains an uphill task. It sets up a season shaped by village politics, distrust and shortages that keep testing rural healthcare.

Did you know 94-year-old Singeetham Srinivasa Rao wrapped Sing Geetham in 77 days? Director Singeetham Srinivasa Rao completed Sing Geetham in 77 shooting days at the age of 94. The film's remote direction, health setback and all-sung narrative underline its unusual production.

general news.headline: IAF aircraft crashes at Assam's Jorhat base, status of crew not knownAn Indian Air Force AN-32 aircraft met with an accident while landing in Assam, which could have triggered a fire. With firefighting operations underway, questions remain over casualties and what led to the incident.

Imports under the price cap: Finland Minister defends India's Russian oil buysAs questions over India's Russian oil purchases resurface, Finland's foreign minister unexpectedly comes to New Delhi's defence, while S Jaishankar delivers a sharp response on energy security, sanctions and Western hypocrisy.

Why US govt banned all foreigners from using Anthropic Claude Fable 5 & MythosThe US has forced Anthropic to block access to its most advanced AI models for all foreign nationals, including Indians. But why is Washington getting possessive about cutting-edge AI, and what does it mean for countries like India that still rely heavily on foreign technology? Lets take a closer look.

Anxiety, uncertainty, and sleepless nights: What Kota told usHandcrafted ByOur Editors India Today travelled to Kota to listen, not just to toppers and teachers, but to the anxieties, hopes and unanswered questions that now define India's coaching capital.

Live in Delhi and use over 500 units of electricity? Your power bill could go upA new electricity surcharge has been approved in Delhi, changing how power costs are recovered every month. While many subsidised consumers may be protected, others could soon see a noticeable increase in their bills.

Brazil's ethanol lesson for India: A Rs 20 difference may not be enoughWith the launch of Delhi's inaugural ethanol fuel pump, India took its first step into the E85 era. However, Brazil's experience shows that lower pump rates alone may not guarantee widespread consumer adoption.

Who will make India rich?Elon Musk's post on India's falling birth rate has renewed focus on the country's sub-replacement fertility trend. The debate now centres on whether India can turn rapid ageing into a silver dividend before its demographic window closes.

Not supposed to say that: Jaishankar's quip draws hilarious reaction from Finnish FMA routine media interaction in Finland turned into a viral moment after a quick exchange between S Jaishankar and his Finnish counterpart. What followed drew laughter in the room and plenty of attention online.

Am I hiding anything? Abhishek slams search at home; Mamata rushes to nephew's aidShalbani police searched Abhishek Banerjee's Kalighat residence for more than four hours in a case linked to his aide Sumit Roy. The move came as the Trinamool leader faced court scrutiny, CID questioning and a public attack from Kalyan Banerjee.

FDs, SCSS or mutual funds? The best way to invest Rs 50 lakh after retirementRetiring with Rs 50 lakh may sound reassuring, but the real challenge begins after retirement: how do you turn that lump sum into a steady monthly income without running out of money?

This startup wants to drop Indian astronauts from edge of space on a balloonRed Balloon Aerospace says its high-altitude balloons can test capsules and train crews before space missions. The company sees the stratosphere as a low-cost way to support Gaganyaan and future lunar plans.

Another Sooryavanshi prodigy? Vaibhav's 10-year-old brother smashes a centuryWhile Vaibhav Sooryavanshi continues to impress for India A in Sri Lanka, his 10-year-old brother Aashirwad has made headlines back home with a century in a local practice match in Bihar.

I Love the Inflation: A Tour of Trump's Private DictionaryUS President Donald Trump has spent years devaluing language the way inflation devalues currency. Words that once carried fixed meanings emerge from his speeches lighter, cheaper and less reliable. From tariffs to ceasefires, deals to patriotism, every definition bends until it serves the man speaking it.
Waking up at 3 am every night? Your body is dropping a big hintIt may be an indication that something is interfering with restful sleep. Is it stress, lifestyle habits or an underlying medical issue?

El Nino has arrived: What is Indian Ocean Dipole that can save monsoon?How or whether the Indian Ocean Dipole develops remains to be seen. But if one looks at history, the climate phenomenon does offer hope.

I watched the Yogasana World Championships and realised my dad was right all alongA trip to the World Yogasana Championship showed how a new sport started its journey into find recognition and a city trying to show its class to be India's new sports hub. By the time 2036 comes in, will Yogasana and Ahmedabad truly live through the test of time?

Sejal Pawar not a doctor yet: KEM Hospital orders probe into cadaver remarkKEM Hospital has launched an inquiry against MBBS student Sejal Pawar over her controversial remarks about male cadavers during comedian Pranit More's show, with a report expected within days.

Skyroot's Vikram-I getting ready for maiden launch at Sriharikota. See first picsSkyroot Aerospace has completed Stage 2 integration of the Vikram-1 rocket at Sriharikota. India's first private orbital rocket is inching toward its maiden launch in 2026. Here is what the Kalam-250 motor actually does and why it matters.

25 years later, I watched Lagaan for the first time. It still hits for sixTwenty-five years later, I watched Lagaan and it still feels astonishingly alive. What could have easily become a relic of another era remains a deeply moving story about hope, community, nation-building and standing up against impossible odds.

Techie, suffering from insomnia, jumps from 6th floor with infant; baby survivesIsha Sahu allegedly jumped from a residential building carrying her infant. The baby slipped during the fall, survived with minor injuries and was hospitalised.

Ranchi twins Karan, Arjun missing for 12 days; family claims police inactionTwin brothers Karan and Arjun have remained missing from Ranchi's Dhurwa area for 12 days, with a mysterious call from Karnataka claiming they were hospitalised after an accident adding a new twist to the case.

Geneva likely venue for US-Iran peace deal signing; Tehran denies reportsGeneva is being considered as a possible venue for signing a US-Iran peace agreement, but Tehran has dismissed claims that a deal will be signed on Sunday, saying no final decision has been made yet.

sports.headline: Can Carlo Ancelotti end Brazil's 24-year wait or is World Cup a different game? Carlo Ancelotti has conquered club football for more than two decades, but the Brazil job presents a different challenge altogether. As the Selecao begin their World Cup campaign, the Italian is chasing both a sixth star and a place in Brazilian football folklore.

IND vs AFG Live, 1st ODI: Rain delays toss in Dharamshala India (IND) vs Afghanistan (AFG) 1st ODI Live Score: Rain delayed the toss ahead of the opening ODI between India and Afghanistan at the HPCA Stadium in Dharamshala on Saturday, with persistent showers keeping players off the field. The weather had already emerged as a major concern after an orange alert was issued for the region, with forecasts predicting thunderstorms, rain and possible hail.

Bangladesh spinner Nayeem Hasan alleges assault by policemen in Chattogram Bangladesh cricketer Nayeem Hasan has accused police officers of assaulting him in Chattogram on Friday night, with the alleged incident drawing strong reactions from fellow players and leading the Bangladesh Cricket Board (BCB) to demand an investigation.

Highlights: PV Sindhu loses Australian Open semifinal to Akane Yamaguchi Australian Open 2026 semifinal live score: That's the end of a decent run in the Super 500 tournament for PV Sindhu. She lost to third seed and reigning world champion Akane Yamaguchi 20-22, 12-20. The wait for an appearance in the final this year continues for the two-time Olympic medalist.

Another Sooryavanshi prodigy? Vaibhav's 10-year-old brother smashes a century While Vaibhav Sooryavanshi continues to impress for India A in Sri Lanka, his 10-year-old brother Aashirwad has made headlines back home with a century in a local practice match in Bihar.

By the time 2036 comes in, will Yogasana and Ahmedabad truly live through the test of time?

Irreparable loss: Manu Bhaker mourns death of Jaspal Rana, her friend and coach Double Olympic medallist Manu Bhaker paid tribute to legendary shooter Jaspal Rana after his sudden death at 49. Her grief recalled a partnership that reshaped her career and underpinned her Paris Olympic medals.

Tom Cruise to Beckham: Stars who stole the spotlight at USA's World Cup opener Tom Cruise, David Beckham, Brad Pitt and Leonardo DiCaprio were among a host of celebrities at SoFi Stadium in Los Angeles as the United States kicked off their FIFA World Cup 2026 campaign against Paraguay, adding a touch of Hollywood glamour to one of the tournament's most anticipated opening-week fixtures.

FIFA World Cup: England camp hit by theft in USA days before opener vs Croatia England's build-up to the 2026 FIFA World Cup has been disrupted after training equipment was stolen while being transported to the team's base in Kansas City, just days before their opening match against Croatia.

World Cup: Balogun's brace helps USA hammer Paraguay 4-1 in Los Angeles The United States made a dream start to their home FIFA World Cup campaign, brushing aside Paraguay 4-1 in their Group D opener at SoFi Stadium in Inglewood, California, on Friday.

Rohit Sharma has a point to prove as India begin their road to the 2027 World Cup India vs Afghanistan, 1st ODI preview: With Virat Kohli absent and the selectors watching, Rohit Sharma's second act begins in Dharamsala, where India's World Cup preparations meet their first big question: who belongs in the team?

Kim Kardashian gifts Kimi Antonelli a towel after viral Monaco GP mix-upKim Kardashian has made amends with Mercedes youngster Kimi Antonelli after accidentally using his towel during the Monaco Grand Prix weekend. The reality star responded to the viral moment by gifting the Italian driver a personalised replacement ahead of the Spanish GP.

Neymar to miss Brazil's World Cup opener against MoroccoFIFA World Cup 2026: Brazil will begin their campaign without Neymar after Carlo Ancelotti confirmed the forward is not fit for the Morocco clash. However, the Brazil coach offered an encouraging update, saying the star forward is progressing well in his recovery.

Dembele shuts Mbappe critics down ahead of France's World Cup openerFIFA World Cup 2026: Ousmane Dembele has thrown his support behind France captain Kylian Mbappe ahead of the World Cup , calling the criticism aimed at the Real Madrid star "very unfair." The Ballon d'Or winner insisted people often forget that Mbappe is "still a human being" despite the constant scrutiny surrounding him.

Dani Wyatt-Hodge, Freya Kemp fuel England's rout in Women's T20 World Cup openerDanni Wyatt-Hodge's unbeaten century powered England to a record 219/1 before Freya Kemp's four-wicket haul sealed an 87-run victory over Sri Lanka in the Women's T20 World Cup opener.

Cyle Larin rescues Canada in 1-1 draw with Bosnia at home World Cup openerFIFA World Cup 2026: Super-sub Cyle Larin scored in the 78th minute as Canada salvaged a 1-1 draw against Bosnia and Herzegovina in Toronto. The point kept the co-hosts steady in their Group B opener after they dominated large stretches without injured captain Alphonso Davies.

Ronaldo's dream, Yamal's moment and a World Cup wide open for the takingFormer India captain Bhaichung Bhutia shares his World Cup 2026 favourites, backs Cristiano Ronaldo's final quest for glory, discusses Lamine Yamal's rise and explains why India still lags behind Asia's best.

Canadian teen Victoria Mboko out of Wimbledon due to knee injury at Queen'sVictoria Mboko's Wimbledon hopes have been dashed after a knee ligament injury at Queen's Club. The Canadian teenager's withdrawal also ends her much-anticipated doubles partnership with Serena Williams.

FIFA explain empty seats after South Korea vs Czechia crowd sparks debateFIFA defended its attendance figure after television images showed empty seats during South Korea's 2-1 win over the Czech Republic in Guadalajara. The explanation has renewed scrutiny of ticket prices, dynamic pricing and fan access at the World Cup.

Thomas Partey ruled out of Ghana's opening game as Canada denies visa applicationFIFA World Cup 2026: Ghana midfielder Thomas Partey will miss the Black Stars' opening World Cup match against Panama after Canadian authorities refused his visa application, FIFA confirmed, though he remains eligible for Ghana's remaining group-stage fixtures.

Nora Fatehi kickstarts FIFA World Cup in Canada with grand opening ceremonyFIFA World Cup 2026: Canadian-born Bollywood superstar Nora Fatehi returned home in style, headlining Toronto's World Cup opening ceremony and debuting her official anthem Siir Siir, turning BMO Field into a celebration before football officially took over.

USA vs Paraguay Highlights: USA thrash Paraguay 4-1 in Los AngelesUnited States (USA) vs Paraguay (PAR) Highlights, FIFA World Cup 2026: The United States kicked off their 2026 FIFA World Cup campaign in style, cruising to a 4-1 win over Paraguay in Los Angeles. Mauricio Pochettino's side stormed into a 3-0 first-half lead thanks to an own goal from Damian Bobadilla and a Folarin Balogun brace, before Paraguay grabbed a late consolation. Earlier in the day, co-hosts Canada were held to a 1-1 draw by Bosnia and Herzegovina in their opening Group B encounter.

Women's T20 World Cup Highlights: England take 87-run win in tournament openerEngland (ENG-W) vs Sri Lanka (SL-W) Highlights, Women's T20 World Cup: England stamped their authority on the tournament with a commanding win over Sri Lanka, as Sophie Ecclestone struck twice in the final over to bowl the opposition out and complete a clinical all-round performance.

England vs Sri Lanka Live Score, Women's T20 World Cup: Stars ready to light up opening ceremonyEngland (ENG-W) vs Sri Lanka (SL-W) Live Score, Women's T20 World Cup: Excitement is building as the Women's T20 World Cup opening ceremony is set to begin, with fans filling the venue ahead of the tournament's grand launch.

Why Donald Trump won't attend USA's FIFA World Cup opener vs ParaguayUS President Donald Trump will not attend the United States' FIFA World Cup opener against Paraguay, despite the country co-hosting the tournament with Mexico and Canada.

Messi has done it, why not Ronaldo? Bhaichung Bhutia picks his FIFA World Cup favouritesSpeaking to India Today's Harshit Ahuja, former India captain Bhaichung Bhutia picked his favourites for the 2026 FIFA World Cup, while stressing that choosing a single frontrunner is far from straightforward. The football legend said as many as seven or eight teams are genuine contenders and capable of lifting the sport's most prestigious trophy.

Rohit's fitness, Virat's replacement: Morkel spells out India's plans for Afghan ODIsSpeaking to the media on the eve of India's ODI series against Afghanistan in Dharamsala, bowling coach Morne Morkel dismissed concerns over Rohit Sharma's fitness and outlined the team's options to replace the injured Virat Kohli at No. 3.

Neeraj Chopra to miss Doha Diamond League, yet to recover from back injuryNeeraj Chopra will miss the Doha Diamond League on June 19 as he continues recovering from a back injury in Switzerland. His absence comes a year after his 90.23m national record in Doha, where another elite javelin field is set to compete.

Virat Kohli congratulates retiring Williamson: Well done mate, life's only just begunVirat Kohli paid an emotional tribute to Kane Williamson following the New Zealand great's retirement from international cricket, praising their friendship and celebrating his remarkable legacy.

The Fab Four had giants. Kane Williamson is the one they all lovedHe averaged nearly 50 across formats, won a World Test Championship, and came agonisingly close to a World Cup. But Kane Williamson's greatest contribution to cricket was something no scorecard ever captured.

India vs Afghanistan 1st ODI: When and where to watch, squads and key updatesIndia move from a one-sided Test win into a three-match ODI series against Afghanistan. The series opens a new season for Shubman Gill's side and gives Afghanistan a first bilateral ODI shot at India.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
